meta.title: Mohanad Zahed — Frontend Architect
meta.description: Mohanad Zahed — Software Architect Frontend / Senior Frontend Engineer. Angular, React, micro-frontends, headless commerce.

hero.name: Mohanad Zahed
hero.title: Frontend Architect
hero.tagline: +8 years of passion for clean architecture, modern code, and workflows

about.heading: About me
about.body: I'm a Frontend Software Architect with a passion for clean architecture and modern code. For over 8 years I've delivered Angular applications for insurance, telecom, retail, and the German public sector. My focus has been on modularization, micro-frontends, and migrating legacy systems to headless platforms. Full-stack experience lets me speak the same language as backend teams and own CI/CD end-to-end, from commit to release. My profile is rounded out by solid AI-augmented development skills that I use to ship more efficiently. Years of working in agile and Scrum environments give me a firm grasp of process, quality, and steady delivery.
facts: Languages — Arabic (native) · English (negotiation level) · German (negotiation level)
Degree — 03/2014 B.Sc. Informatics, Amman (officially recognized in Germany)

certificates.heading: Certificates
certificates.subhead: Continuing education in architecture, delivery, and testing.

experience.heading: Where I've shipped.
experience.subhead: Three companies, twelve projects, eight years.

step.name: STEP — Master Data Management System
step.description:
STEP is the central master data system of the German Federal Employment Agency (Bundesagentur für Arbeit) — capturing and maintaining employer and individual records. It acts as the central system for nearly every BA IT process that depends on master data, and exposes that data through REST endpoints, Kafka events, file interfaces, and callable APIs.

STEP also owns employer-data management for social-security reporting, plus the MAZ component (proof and reporting of contribution-free periods for the pension system). Delivery combines BA IT standards with strict data-protection, data-security, and accessibility requirements.
tasks: Locating, analysing, and resolving software defects
Supporting the production of functional and solution-design specifications
Maintaining Continuous Integration / Continuous Delivery
Applying the DevSecOps approach
Applying agile practices
Taking on 3rd-level support
Test analysis, plus authoring and maintaining manual component, system, and end-to-end tests
Implementing new REST endpoints and evolving existing interfaces and Kafka-based event flows
Evolving GUI components
Conducting code reviews and refactoring to lift code quality
Ensuring accessibility compliance

svlfg.name: B2B Insurance Customer Portal Modernization
svlfg.description:
Modernization and restructuring of an existing B2B insurance customer portal originally built on SAP Commerce (Hybris). The monolithic architecture was replaced by a decoupled headless approach, with the frontend rebuilt in Angular. The backend initially remained on SAP Hybris / Spring Boot, with custom controllers exposing OpenAPI-aligned RESTful endpoints. Delivery followed Scrum in monthly sprints.

Long-term goal: incremental migration off SAP Commerce onto a modern JHipster-based stack, since SAP Commerce was being used here purely as a technical base with no real e-commerce functionality. This delivers a significant reduction in licensing cost together with improved maintainability, frontend/backend independence, and platform future-proofing.
tasks: Migrating the Angular application into an Nx monorepo to structure and share libraries across multiple applications
Establishing and enforcing frontend architecture and code-style best practices
Supporting and technically mentoring the internal frontend team
Conducting code reviews and giving technical feedback on merge requests
Working closely with the backend team on integration and consumption of OpenAPI-driven REST endpoints
Implementing UI components against the new style guide in close collaboration with design
Ensuring accessibility, responsive design, and adherence to UX/UI guidelines
Running manual component and UI tests
Building GUI components with a strong focus on usability and performance
Documenting source code and UI components
Setting up and configuring the Angular CI pipeline

mobilcom-ecare.name: mobilcom-debitel eCare User Dashboard Relaunch
mobilcom-ecare.description:
Comprehensive relaunch of the existing eCare self-service platform with the goal of a faster, friendlier, and more visually compelling experience. Focus: performance optimization, a new responsive design, and features that give users a clearer view of their contracts, usage, and account details.

The dashboard was rebuilt from the ground up on the existing Angular architecture — an Nx monorepo with a fully GraphQL-driven backend. Functionally extended with personalized content (marketing campaigns, product recommendations, special offers) rendered dynamically and user-aware. Delivered in an agile Scrum setup in close collaboration with UX designers and marketing stakeholders.
tasks: Implementing the new eCare dashboard page showing contracts, data usage, invoices, marketing content, and more
Working with designers and the project manager to realise the desired UI
Authoring and grooming Jira tickets to plan and structure the work
Integrating the new dashboard into the existing Angular app in coordination with the development team
Building in Angular, TypeScript, HTML, and SCSS inside an Nx monorepo
Fetching backend data via GraphQL with a focus on performance and maintainability
Implementing the new UI surfaces in line with the style guide
Ensuring accessibility, responsive design, and UX/UI guideline adherence
Running E2E tests
Building the GUI components with strong attention to usability

emporix.name: Agnostic Integration SDK for Emporix
emporix.description: Development of an agnostic integration SDK that drops into any frontend application and provides a ready-to-use binding to the Emporix e-commerce platform. Technology-neutral by design for maximum flexibility and reusability. The official Alokai integration boilerplate served as the foundation to ensure consistency and maintainability.
tasks: Building TypeScript modules inside the integration SDK to handle login, product, and product-list queries
Implementing and maintaining the SDK as reusable npm packages
Publishing and versioning the packages to the internal GitLab registry
Ensuring framework-agnostic design and easy integration into different frontend applications

haba.name: Online-shop Performance Optimization (HABA Play & HABA Pro)
haba.description: Performance improvement for the HABA Play and HABA Pro online shops while lowering maintenance and runtime cost. The sites suffered from long load times and a heavy dependency on SAP Composable Storefront. At the customer's request, SAP Composable Storefront had to be removed without losing any core functionality.
tasks: Analysing the existing shops to identify load-time bottlenecks and unnecessary JavaScript
Implementing optimizations to reduce load times, including lazy loading
Supporting the removal of SAP Composable Storefront on customer request
Lifting key functionality out into a dedicated, reusable library
Simplifying and reducing the codebase to lower maintenance overhead and bundle size
Working with the development team and stakeholders to ensure a smooth migration without feature loss

ottos-sherpa.name: Modern online shops on SAP Commerce Cloud and Composable Storefront
ottos-sherpa.description: Development of modern online shops for ottos.ch and Sherpa Outdoors on top of SAP Commerce Cloud and SAP Composable Storefront. Migration off the legacy SAP Hybris system onto a headless storefront architecture. A single shared Angular frontend codebase was specialised per brand through distinct themes and configurations. Delivered under Scrum, in close collaboration with Valantic (Zürich), Medienwerft (Hamburg), Team Neusta (Bremen), and Maify GmbH (Mühlingen).
tasks: Frontend project lead, technically supporting Team Neusta and Medienwerft on SAP Composable Storefront
Creating technical sub-tasks and assigning Jira tickets to streamline structure and onboarding
Reviewing code and merge requests from team members while also contributing actively to the code
Tight collaboration and coordination with the backend team, product manager, and product owner
Ensuring adherence to best practices, code standards, and frontend quality
Supporting technical decisions and solution design throughout the project
Implementing UI components against the new style guide in close collaboration with design
Ensuring accessibility, responsive design, and UX/UI guideline adherence
Running manual component and E2E tests
Building GUI components with a strong focus on usability and performance
Setting up and configuring the Angular CI pipeline

landgard.name: B2B Online Shops for Landgard and Ordertage
landgard.description: Development of modern online shops for Landgard and Ordertage on SAP Commerce Cloud and SAP Composable Storefront. A single shared Angular frontend codebase was specialised per brand through distinct themes and configurations. Delivered under Scrum.
tasks: Implementing components and features on SAP Composable Storefront
Tailoring the shared Angular codebase to each brand through configuration and theming
Working closely with the backend team to integrate interfaces and data flows
Aligning with the product owner on translating business requirements into delivery
Participating in sprint planning, reviews, and daily meetings in the Scrum team
Implementing UI components against the new style guide in close collaboration with design
Ensuring accessibility, responsive design, and UX/UI guideline adherence
Building GUI components with a strong focus on usability and performance

spar.name: Online-shop Accessibility Refit (SPAR Hungary & Italy)
spar.description: Accessibility refit of the SPAR Hungary and SPAR Italy online shops. The existing shops were built on SAP Commerce (Hybris) with a JSP frontend that did not meet current digital accessibility requirements. A large portion of the existing code was reworked and refactored to align with applicable accessibility guidelines and deliver a better user experience. Delivered under Scrum.
tasks: Analysing the existing JSP frontend for accessibility issues
Refactoring and adjusting HTML, CSS, and JavaScript to meet accessibility guidelines
Running accessibility tests and using the relevant tooling for quality assurance
Supporting the team in delivering accessibility standards inside the Scrum process

daw.name: 13 Online Shops for the DAW Group
daw.description: Development of a new e-commerce system on SAP Commerce Hybris that allowed users to configure paint colours interactively. The system was designed as the central solution for 13 different online shops, each differing in CMS content, product range, and theme. Delivered inside a Scrum process.
tasks: Implementing frontend features usable across all 13 shops
Working closely with the backend team
Coordinating with the product owner on clarifying business requirements
Adapting components to different CMS content, product data, and themes
Implementing UI components against the new style guide in close collaboration with design

dachdecker.name: Roofers' Online Shop
dachdecker.description: Development of a new online shop on SAP Commerce Hybris, tailored to roofing-trade needs. The shop was individually configured and themed for the roofing industry. Delivered agile under Scrum.
tasks: Evolving the existing shop and shipping new features
Fixing bugs and technical issues in the frontend
Working with the backend team to integrate new functionality
Aligning with the product owner on prioritisation
Implementing UI components against the new style guide in close collaboration with design

frosta.name: FRoSTA Digital Work Instructions
frosta.description: Development of a mobile application that digitally surfaces work and machine instructions. Content (videos, images, PDFs, text) explains each work step visually and clearly to shop-floor staff. Content is maintained through a separate web application with role-based access. Comprised a native Android app, an Angular CMS, and a REST API on JAX-RS backed by Microsoft SQL Server.
tasks: Frontend: building the mobile app on Android Native to surface work instructions with video, images, PDFs, and text
Frontend: building an Angular 6 CMS web app to manage content through authorized users
Frontend: designing a user-friendly UI for both content viewing and authoring
Backend: building RESTful web services on JAX-RS to serve content to the app and CMS
Backend: integrating and managing content via a Microsoft SQL Server database
Backend: ensuring data consistency and performance across frontend and database
Technical alignment with the product owner on delivering the requirements

lsw.name: LSW Warehouse Picking
lsw.description: Development of a mobile application for Android and iOS tablets, used by warehouse staff for picking and quality control. The app scans barcodes, surfaces product information, runs quality checks, and confirms work orders via digital signature. Completed order data flows through the SAP Java Connector (JCo) into the connected SAP ERP system. Built on Ionic in the frontend with JAX-RS web services and Microsoft SQL Server on the backend.
tasks: Frontend: building a cross-platform mobile app on Ionic for Android and iOS
Frontend: integrating the barcode scanner to capture warehouse product data
Frontend: displaying the product-relevant information
Frontend: implementing the digital signature flow to confirm orders
Backend: building RESTful web services on JAX-RS to interface with the SAP ERP system
Backend: connecting to a SQL Server database to manage and store order data
Backend: processing completed orders and forwarding them to the ERP system
Backend: keeping the communication between app and backend stable

contact.headline: Let's build something.
contact.subhead: Open to senior frontend / architecture roles and consulting engagements in Germany and remote.
